name: Airbnb
description: Clean, minimal marketplace UI with generous whitespace, rounded corners, and a warm color palette. Known for its card-based layouts, intuitive search, and multi-step flows that reduce cognitive load.

designSystem.typography: Cereal (custom), clean sans-serif, bold hierarchy — large titles, medium labels, regular body
designSystem.spacing: Generous whitespace, 16-24px padding in cards, 48-64px section spacing
patterns: Card grid with image, title, price, rating — consistent aspect ratios
Floating search bar with segmented inputs (Where, When, Who)
Centered modals with icon + title + description + CTA pattern
Multi-step checkout with numbered accordion sections and sticky summary sidebar
Pill-shaped CTAs with high contrast (black fill, white text)
Subtle badges (Guest favorite) with soft backgrounds
Price breakdown with expandable details
keyTakeaways: Whitespace is a feature, not wasted space — every element breathes
Multi-step forms shown as collapsed accordion, not a wizard — user sees the full journey upfront
Sticky sidebar summary during checkout keeps context visible while filling forms
Modals are centered, simple, and single-purpose — one message, one CTA

screenshots.description: Airbnb homepage with card grid, floating search bar, and centered pricing modal. Shows the clean card layout with image thumbnails, pricing, ratings, and 'Guest favorite' badges.

Airbnb homepage without modal overlay. Full card grid visible with horizontal scroll rows grouped by location (Medellín, Cartagena, Santa Marta). Segmented top navigation (Homes, Experiences, Services).
Airbnb referral modal — 'How it works' with 3 icon+text steps and a single black CTA button. Shows the centered modal pattern with generous padding and clear visual hierarchy.

Airbnb 'Confirm and pay' checkout flow. Left side: numbered accordion steps (1. Log in, 2. Payment, 3. Review). Right side: sticky booking summary with property image, dates, guests, price breakdown, and 'Price is below average' nudge.

name: Hourglass — Modern Healthcare Dashboard
description: A modern healthcare dashboard concept by Paperpillar (Dribbble). Breaks away from typical clinical aesthetics with a soft glassmorphic UI, gradient backgrounds, and a widget-based layout. Demonstrates that healthcare tools can feel warm, approachable, and beautiful without sacrificing information density.

designSystem.typography: Clean sans-serif (likely Inter or similar), large 'Welcome' heading, medium weight for section titles, regular for body text. Doctor name in bold serif-like weight for warmth.
designSystem.spacing: Widget-based grid with 16-20px gaps. Cards have 16-24px internal padding. Generous section spacing with clear visual grouping.
patterns: Left sidebar navigation with icon + label, active state highlighted with filled background
Hero card with doctor photo, appointment details, and quick-action icons below
Widget grid layout — independent cards for different functions (Find Doctor, Health Tips, Results, Queue)
Icon grid for doctor specialties — circular icon buttons in a clean grid with labels
Appointment queue widget with dropdown selector and compact table (queue, urgent, ETA)
Health tips as horizontal card list with illustration + title + description
Result & History as a compact timeline/list with date markers
Quick-action icon bar (Pharmacy, My Queue, Book, Doctors, Schedule) with circular icons
Glassmorphic card surfaces with subtle blur and semi-transparent backgrounds
keyTakeaways: Healthcare doesn't have to look clinical — soft gradients and rounded corners make it approachable
Widget-based dashboard lets each section be independent and scannable at a glance
The doctor specialty icon grid is a great pattern for product category selection — we can adapt this for medical supply categories
Appointment queue widget maps directly to our order queue — compact table with status indicators
Quick-action icon bar reduces the need for deep navigation — most common tasks are one click away

screenshots.description: Hourglass healthcare dashboard by Paperpillar. Full dashboard view showing: left sidebar nav (Dashboard, Appointments, Messages, Pharmacy, Laboratory, Inpatient, Support, Settings), hero card with Dr. Haylie Philips and appointment details, 'Find a doctor' specialty icon grid (General, Pediatrics, Gynecology, Internal Medicine, Dermatology, Mental Health, Cardiology, Orthopedics, Infectious Disease), quick-action icon bar, Health Tips cards, Result & History list, and Appointment Queue widget with Dr. James Carter.

name: Pills — Medical Supply E-Commerce
description: A medical supply e-commerce concept with clean product categorization, pill-shaped filter chips, and a warm, approachable aesthetic. Demonstrates how to organize medical products into browsable categories with clear pricing and hover-to-preview interactions.

designSystem.typography: Serif headings (elegant, medical feel) paired with clean sans-serif body text. Large hero text, medium category labels, small product details.
designSystem.spacing: Generous whitespace throughout. Large hero section with floating product images. Card grid with 16-20px gaps. Category pills with comfortable padding.
patterns: Hero section with floating product images on sky-blue background — aspirational, not clinical
Three main category cards (Prescription medicine, Surgical product, Supplements & vitamins) with large images and descriptive subtitles
Pill-shaped category filter chips with icons (Diabetes Care, Vitamins & Supplements, Pain Relief, Allergy, Skin Care, Mental Wellness, Bone & Joint)
Product card grid with image, name, price, and quantity selector
Special offers carousel with horizontal scroll and hover-to-expand product detail popup
Hover popup showing product image, name, price, and 'Shop Now' CTA — progressive disclosure
Expandable accordion sections for offer categories (Immunity Sale, Vitamins Offers, Skin Deals)
Top navigation with Home, Request Order, Special Offer, Search, Account, Cart
keyTakeaways: Category pill chips with icons are perfect for product type filtering in our order form — adapt for HCPCS categories or product lines
The three main category cards pattern maps directly to our product types (compression garments, surgical supplies, etc.)
Hover-to-preview on product cards reduces clicks — useful for showing product details while selecting items in the order form
Warm beige/sage palette proves medical supply tools don't need to be sterile blue — approachable design builds trust
'Request Order' as a top-level nav item is exactly our primary use case

screenshots.description: Pills full-page overview showing hero section with floating product bottles, three category cards (Prescription medicine, Surgical product, Supplements & vitamins), pill-shaped category chips with icons, product card grid with pricing, special offers section with hover product preview, and social/CTA footer.

Pills special offers page showing horizontal product carousel with hover popup (product image, MedRelief name, $28.88 price, 'Shop Now' CTA), expandable accordion offer categories (Immunity Sale, Vitamins Offers, Skin Deals), and help contact info.

name: Dentexa — Clinical Operations Dashboard
description: A dental clinic management dashboard with comprehensive financial and operational metrics. Features a well-organized left sidebar with grouped navigation sections, revenue/expense visualizations, patient data, popular treatments, and inventory management with low-stock alerts.

designSystem.typography: Clean sans-serif (Inter-like). Large bold numbers for KPIs ($487,650). Medium weight for section headers. Regular for labels and data.
designSystem.spacing: Widget-based grid with 16-20px gaps between cards. Cards have 20-24px internal padding. Sidebar has clear section grouping with dividers.
patterns: Left sidebar with grouped nav sections: Clinic (Dashboard, Appointment, Patients, Treatments, Staff list), Finance (Accounts, Sales, Purchases, Payment Method), Physical Asset (Stocks, Peripherals), plus Report and Customer Support
Revenue line chart with 12-month view, total cash KPI ($487,650 ↑ 18.3%), and tooltip showing monthly detail
Expenses donut chart with category breakdown (Staff Salaries 42%, Medical Supplies 25%, Facility Rent 15%, Equipment M. 11%, Marketing 5%, Utilities 2%)
Financial Overview bar chart with monthly income vs. costs comparison
Patient Data widget with first-time vs. follow-up visit split (156/164, 48.75%/51.25%)
Popular Treatments list with star ratings (Health Screening 4.8, Consultation 4.5, Vaccination 4.9)
Inventory Status with total value ($284,900), total items (1,547 units), stacked status bar (Available/Low Stock/Out of Stock), and Low Stock alert table with item, quantity, and Order action button
keyTakeaways: Grouped sidebar navigation (Clinic/Finance/Physical Asset) is perfect for our tool — we can group by Orders, Billing, Products, Admin
Inventory Status with low-stock alerts maps directly to our product catalog management needs
Revenue vs. Expenses visualization pattern works for our margin/cost analysis views
Patient data split (first-time vs. follow-up) can adapt to new orders vs. reorders
The dashboard density is right — comprehensive without being overwhelming. Good balance for our order management overview.

screenshots.description: Dentexa full dashboard view. Left sidebar with grouped navigation (Clinic: Dashboard/Appointment/Patients/Treatments/Staff list, Finance: Accounts/Sales/Purchases/Payment Method, Physical Asset: Stocks/Peripherals, plus Report/Customer Support). Main area shows: Revenue line chart ($487,650 total, 12-month view), Expenses donut chart ($256,320 with category breakdown), Financial Overview bar chart, Patient Data (156/164 visits), Popular Treatments with ratings, and Inventory Status with low stock alerts (Surgical Masks, Latex Gloves).

name: Xenityhealth — Healthcare Analytics Dashboard
description: A modern healthcare analytics dashboard with prominent KPI cards, revenue trends, diagnosis distribution, and daily schedule timeline. Clean white design with blue/orange accent colors. Two layout variants show slightly different spacing and scroll behaviors.

designSystem.typography: Clean sans-serif. Extra-large bold numbers for KPI cards (6025, 4152, 5948, 5626). Medium weight for section titles. Sparkline micro-charts inline with KPI values.
designSystem.spacing: 4-column KPI card row at top. 2-column layout below (wide chart left, narrow widget right). Cards with 20-24px padding. 16px gaps between widgets.
patterns: Top KPI card row with 4 metric cards: each has icon, label, large number, sparkline trend, and percentage change badge (green ↑ or red ↓)
Revenue overview area chart with time period toggle (1 Year / 6 Months / 2 Months / 1 Month) and monthly tooltip ($47,500)
Avg Diagnose donut chart with total patient count center (640) and category breakdown (Neurology 120, Oncology 50, Urology 24)
Tab navigation within dashboard (Overview, Medical Reports, Patients Overview, Diagnose)
Today's Schedule horizontal timeline with avatar + appointment type cards placed at time slots
Latest Visits list with patient avatar, name, specialty, date, and time — compact row format
Left sidebar navigation with icons + labels (Dashboard, Patient, Message with notification badge, Appointment, Medical Record, Analytics, Billing, Settings)
keyTakeaways: KPI cards with sparkline trends are excellent for our order overview — adapt for: Active Orders, Pending Approvals, Revenue This Month, Orders Shipped
Tab navigation within the dashboard page keeps related views accessible without deep navigation — useful for Orders/Billing/Products views
The schedule timeline pattern can be adapted for order processing timeline or shipment tracking
Orange accent for alerts/critical items creates clear visual priority without red-alarm fatigue
Latest Visits list pattern maps perfectly to our recent orders list with patient, product, status, and date

screenshots.description: Xenityhealth dashboard variant 1. Left sidebar nav (Dashboard, Patient, Message with red badge, Appointment, Medical Record, Analytics, Billing, Settings). Top KPI row: Patients 6025 (↑68.95%), New This Week 4152 (↑471%), Critical Alerts 5948 (↓42.06%), Appointments 5626 (↑27.41%). Revenue overview area chart ($138,500 avg/month), Avg Diagnose donut (640 total), Today's Schedule timeline, Latest Visits list.

Xenityhealth dashboard variant 2 — same layout with slightly different scroll position showing more of the Latest Visits list (Esther Howard, Eleanor Pena, Brooklyn Simmons, Cameron Williamson). Demonstrates the full-height sidebar and consistent card spacing across the dashboard.
